Address 6 review comment(s) on PR #498: feat: Competitive Intel Page Redesign - Incorporate Kvoth Weekly Scans ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/clawd/slideheroes-internal-tools` 2. `git fetch origin && git checkout sophie/issue-497 && git pull origin sophie/issue-497` 3. Read the full PR: `gh pr view 498 --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools` 4. Address each review comment below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "fix: address review comments on PR #498"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-497` ## Review Comments to Address ### Comment 1 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/app/api/kvoth/latest-scan/route.ts:20) ```diff @@ -0,0 +1,54 @@ +import { NextResponse } from "next/server" + +import { prisma } from "@/lib/db" +import { parseKvothScan } from "@/lib/kvoth/parse-scan" + +export const dynamic = "force-dynamic" + +export async function GET() { + try { + // Find the most recent document tagged with "Competitive Intel" + const document = await prisma.document.findFirst({ + where: { + tags: { + contains: "Competitive Intel", + }, + }, + orderBy: { + updatedAt: ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **Constrain the lookup to Kvoth scan documents.** The requirement here is the latest markdown scan under `artifacts/kvoth/`, but this query selects any document whose tags contain `"Competitive Intel"`. A newer non-Kvoth doc with the same tag will win and the dashboard will show the wrong scan.
🛠️ Proposed fix ```diff const document = await prisma.document.findFirst({ where: { tags: { contains: "Competitive Intel", }, + path: { + contains: "artifacts/kvoth/", + }, }, orderBy: { updatedAt: "desc", }, + select: { + id: true, + path: true, + content: true, + updatedAt: true, + }, }) ```
📝 Committable suggestion > ‼️ **IMPORTANT** > Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements. ```suggestion const document = await prisma.document.findFirst({ where: { tags: { contains: "Competitive Intel", }, path: { contains: "artifacts/kvoth/", }, }, orderBy: { updatedAt: "desc", }, select: { id: true, path: true, content: true, updatedAt: true, }, }) ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/app/api/kvoth/latest-scan/route.ts` around lines 11 - 20, The query in prisma.document.findFirst currently only filters by tags contains "Competitive Intel" and may return non-Kvoth docs; update the where clause to also restrict to Kvoth scan artifacts by adding an additional predicate that matches the document path/source to the Kvoth folder (e.g., require the document.path or document.key to startWith "artifacts/kvoth/" or require source === "kvoth"), keeping the orderBy updatedAt: "desc" so the latest Kvoth markdown is chosen; modify the where object next to tags to include this path/source constraint. ```
### Comment 2 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/app/api/kvoth/latest-scan/route.ts:52) ```diff @@ -0,0 +1,54 @@ +import { NextResponse } from "next/server" + +import { prisma } from "@/lib/db" +import { parseKvothScan } from "@/lib/kvoth/parse-scan" + +export const dynamic = "force-dynamic" + +export async function GET() { + try { + // Find the most recent document tagged with "Competitive Intel" + const document = await prisma.document.findFirst({ + where: { + tags: { + contains: "Competitive Intel", + }, + }, + orderBy: { + updatedAt: ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **Don't return raw exception text from a routable API.** Line 50 sends `err.message` back to the caller. That can expose Prisma/model details during failures; keep the full error in server logs and return a generic 500 payload instead.
🛡️ Proposed fix ```diff } catch (err) { + console.error("Failed to fetch latest Kvoth scan", err) return NextResponse.json( - { error: "Failed to fetch latest scan", details: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) }, + { error: "Failed to fetch latest scan" }, { status: 500 } ) } ```
Based on learnings: Do not expose sensitive data.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/app/api/kvoth/latest-scan/route.ts` around lines 48 - 52, The catch block currently returns err.message to the client via NextResponse.json which can leak sensitive internals; instead log the full error server-side (using the existing logger or console.error) within the catch and return a generic JSON error payload (e.g., { error: "Failed to fetch latest scan" }) with status 500; update the NextResponse.json call in the catch to remove details from the response while preserving detailed logging before returning. ```
### Comment 3 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/app/competitive-intel/page.tsx:34) ```diff @@ -10,6 +10,29 @@ import { Card } from "@/components/ui/card" import { prisma } from "@/lib/db" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" +type KvothScanData = { + hasScan: boolean + lastScanDate: string | null + alerts: Array<{ emoji: string; message: string }> + statusDashboard: Array<{ name: string; status: string; trend: string; keyChanges: string }> + pricingChanges: Array<{ competitor: string; change: string; details: string }> + recommendations: Array<{ text: string }> + documentId: st ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **The `localhost` fallback makes scan loading environment-dependent.** If `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` is missing outside local dev, Line 26 fetches `http://localhost:3000/api/kvoth/latest-scan`, the request fails, and the page quietly drops the Kvoth data. Because this page already talks to Prisma directly, the safer path is to share the scan lookup as a server helper and call it here instead of routing through HTTP.
🛠️ Proposed refactor ```diff +import { getLatestKvothScan } from "@/lib/kvoth/get-latest-scan" ... async function getLatestScan(): Promise { - try { - const res = await fetch(`${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || "http://localhost:3000"}/api/kvoth/latest-scan`, { - next: { revalidate: 60 }, - }) - if (!res.ok) return null - return res.json() as Promise - } catch { - return null - } + return getLatestKvothScan() } ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/app/competitive-intel/page.tsx` around lines 24 - 34, The fetch-based getLatestScan function uses a NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL fallback to http://localhost:3000 which breaks outside local dev; replace the HTTP call with a direct server-side helper that queries Prisma (e.g., move the API logic into a server helper named getLatestKvothScan or similar) and have getLatestScan call that helper directly and return KvothScanData | null, updating any imports/exports so page.tsx uses the server helper instead of fetch to avoid environment-dependent network requests. ```
### Comment 4 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/app/competitive-intel/page.tsx:411) ```diff @@ -267,6 +311,107 @@ export default async function CompetitiveIntelPage() { ))} )} + + {/* Kvoth Scan Pricing Changes */} + {kvothScan?.pricingChanges && kvothScan.pricingChanges.length > 0 && ( +
+
PRICING CHANGES
+
+ {kvot ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **The trend legend doesn't match the colors the table renders.** `getTrendColor("accelerating")` is green and `getTrendColor("growing")` is blue, but the legend labels the green up-arrow as “Growing” and never explains the blue state. That will mislead users as soon as either trend appears in the scan table.
🎨 Suggested fix ```diff - Growing + Accelerating + + + Growing Declining ```
📝 Committable suggestion > ‼️ **IMPORTANT** > Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements. ```suggestion Accelerating Growing Declining Flat ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/app/competitive-intel/page.tsx` around lines 404 - 411, The legend colors/labels need to match the same mapping used by getTrendColor so users aren’t misled: update the three legend entries (the spans that currently show ArrowUp, ArrowDown, ArrowUpDown and the labels "Growing"/"Declining"/"Flat") to use the exact color classes and label names returned/expected by getTrendColor (e.g., the blue class used for "growing" if getTrendColor("growing") is blue, and the green class used for "accelerating" if that’s green) or alternatively rename labels to "growing"/"accelerating"/"flat" to match the keys used by getTrendColor; ensure the ArrowUp/ArrowDown/ArrowUpDown icons use the same tailwind classes as getTrendColor outputs so the legend and scan table colors are consistent. ```
### Comment 5 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/lib/kvoth/parse-scan.ts:104) ```diff @@ -0,0 +1,230 @@ +export interface KvothAlert { + emoji: string + message: string +} + +export interface CompetitorStatus { + name: string + status: string + trend: string + keyChanges: string +} + +export interface PricingChange { + competitor: string + change: string + details: string +} + +export interface Recommendation { + text: string +} + +export interface KvothScanResult { + lastScanDate: string | null + alerts: KvothAlert[] + statusDashboard: CompetitorStatus[] + pricingC ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ **Status rows never reach the parser.** Line 84 continues on every non-separator `| ... |` row, so the loop never reaches the data-row branch on Line 93. `statusDashboard` will always be empty and the new Competitive Status table never renders.
🛠️ Proposed fix ```diff function parseStatusTable(tableContent: string): CompetitorStatus[] { const competitors: CompetitorStatus[] = [] const lines = tableContent.split("\n") + let headerSeen = false for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) { const line = lines[i].trim() - // Find header row - if (line.startsWith("|") && !line.includes("---")) { - // Skip header row - continue - } + if (!line.startsWith("|")) continue + if (!headerSeen) { + headerSeen = true + continue + } // Skip separator rows if (line.includes("---")) continue - // Parse data rows - if (line.startsWith("|")) { - const cells = line.split("|").filter((c) => c.trim()) - if (cells.length >= 2) { - const name = cells[0]?.trim() || "" - const status = cells[1]?.trim() || "" - const trend = cells[2]?.trim() || "" - const keyChanges = cells[3]?.trim() || "" - - if (name && name.toLowerCase() !== "competitor" && name.toLowerCase() !== "name") { - competitors.push({ name, status, trend, keyChanges }) - } - } - } + const cells = line.split("|").filter((c) => c.trim()) + if (cells.length >= 2) { + const name = cells[0]?.trim() || "" + const status = cells[1]?.trim() || "" + const trend = cells[2]?.trim() || "" + const keyChanges = cells[3]?.trim() || "" + + if (name && name.toLowerCase() !== "competitor" && name.toLowerCase() !== "name") { + competitors.push({ name, status, trend, keyChanges }) + } + } } return competitors } ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/lib/kvoth/parse-scan.ts` around lines 83 - 104, The loop is skipping every pipe-delimited row because the header check (if (line.startsWith("|") && !line.includes("---")) continue) unconditionally continues; update parse-scan.ts to only skip the actual header row (not all rows) by either: 1) detecting header text (e.g., line.toLowerCase().includes("competitor") || line.toLowerCase().includes("name") and line.toLowerCase().includes("status")) and continuing only in that case, or 2) introduce a boolean flag (e.g., seenHeader) that skips the first matching header row and then parses subsequent |...| rows; ensure you still skip separator rows (line.includes("---")) and then allow the data-row branch to populate the competitors array (competitors.push({...})). ```
### Comment 6 by @coderabbitai[bot] **Actionable comments posted: 5**

In `@app/src/app/competitive-intel/page.tsx`: - Around line 24-34: The fetch-based getLatestScan function uses a NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL fallback to http://localhost:3000 which breaks outside local dev; replace the HTTP call with a direct server-side helper that queries Prisma (e.g., move the API logic into a server helper named getLatestKvothScan or similar) and have getLatestScan call that helper directly and return KvothScanData | null, updating any imports/exports so page.tsx uses the server helper instead of fetch to avoid environment-dependent network requests. - Around line 404-411: The legend colors/labels need to match the same mapping used by getTrendColor so users aren’t misled: update the three legend entries (the spans that currently show ArrowUp, ArrowDown, ArrowUpDown and the labels "Growing"/"Declining"/"Flat") to use the exact color classes and label names returned/expected by getTrendColor (e.g., the blue class used for "growing" if getTrendColor("growing") is blue, and the green class used for "accelerating" if that’s green) or alternatively rename labels to "growing"/"accelerating"/"flat" to match the keys used by getTrendColor; ensure the ArrowUp/ArrowDown/ArrowUpDown icons use the same tailwind classes as getTrendColor outputs so the legend and scan table colors are consistent. In `@app/src/lib/kvoth/parse-scan.ts`: - Around line 83-104: The loop is skipping every pipe-delimited row because the header check (if (line.startsWith("|") && !line.includes("---")) continue) unconditionally continues; update parse-scan.ts to only skip the actual header row (not all rows) by either: 1) detecting header text (e.g., line.toLowerCase().includes("competitor") || line.toLowerCase().includes("name") and line.toLowerCase().includes("status")) and continuing only in that case, or 2) introduce a boolean flag (e.g., seenHeader) that skips the first matching header row and then parses subsequent |...| rows; ensure you still skip separator rows (line.includes("---")) and then allow the data-row branch to populate the competitors array (competitors.push({...})). ```
